author: redoctober
text: Liverpool 2026/27 Season - Iraola Officially Confirmed6 minutes agoReplyQuoteSelect PostDeselect PostLink to PostMemberGive GiftBack to TopPost by redoctober on6 minutes agoJun 10, 2026 at 6:26amYehezsaid:I would agree with some who were talking in the transfers thread that challenging for the league might be tought next year if we were talking about the 90+ craziness of Klopp and Guardiola. Considering the last two champions got 85 and 84, and even City before didn't break 90, we need to add 5-7 wins only to compete for a title. It's doable.Target for Andoni is trying to squeeze 80 points.I think 80 points would be a very good season.  It's hard to know what other teams are going to do.  Arsenal look like the team to beat.  Chelsea might be stronger I'd imagine.  City probably weaker but who knows.  United might be there or thereabouts unfortunately but hopefully they implode.  A huge amount depends on who we can get in this summer.  If we recruit well with players that complement what we've got, then you'd have to give us a good chance of a good showing.  The frustrating part with how things have gone for us in recent years, is that when other teams have been weak or the league has been poor, we've also had some of our worst seasons.  Like 2016 when Leicester won it.  We were awful too.  When we had Klopp and were one of the best teams on the planet, we had to contend with peak Pep and his bottomless coffers.  Then this season we could have got another title with the way things went if we were any way decent.  With hindsight you'd wonder if we had just gone again with our team from 2025, would we have got close to another title?  All the players would be one year older etc. but it would be another title in the bag.
